北京时间8月17日消息，当地时间周三，锦织圭的经纪人宣布由于右手腕肌腱撕裂，他将退出美网的争夺并提前结束自己的2017赛季。

　　经纪人Olivier van Lindonk在邮件中写道，锦织圭在辛辛那提进行发球练习时，听到右手腕发出“嘭”的声音，他也因此退出了本周辛辛那提大师赛的争夺。随后，锦织圭咨询了两名腕伤专家，并将核磁共振检查结果交由另外3名专家进行商讨，最终做出了赛季报销的决定。

　　据Van Lindonk透露，在“目前这个阶段”，锦织圭选择不对手腕进行手术，他在未来几周将佩戴护具，待消肿后再确定下一步的治疗方案。

　　此前，去年美网冠军瓦林卡以及前世界第一德约科维奇分别因左膝手术和右侧手肘伤病提前结束了自己的2017赛季。锦织圭的这一决定意味着去年美网的四强选手中，将有三人缺席今年的比赛。
　　2014年美网，锦织圭成为了第一个闯入大满贯决赛的日本选手，但在最终的决赛中不敌西里奇无缘冠军。去年同样是在美网，他职业生涯第2次闯入大满贯四强，在半决赛中负于最终的冠军瓦林卡。
　　锦织圭在职业生涯中屡次受到伤病影响，但他此前已连续出战了21站大满贯比赛，上一次缺席还要追溯到2012年的法网。他2017赛季的战绩将定格为30胜13负，其中包含了两次巡回赛决赛经历。

Is serve-and-volley the best way to hold your serve in tennis? If yes, why do only a few players do it?

There are five factors involved in the decline of the serve and volley in men’s professional tennis. (For the women, there have been only a handful of women who had dominant serves and the quickness to cover the net in spite of their reduced reach, so the factors below have made it almost impossible for them to use this tactic on a regular basis.)

#1 Slowing down of the court surfaces and balls (Wimbledon was analyzed as playing slower than a clay court in Quito). Up until the 1990’s, there was enough variation in court and ball speeds to allow players to develop different styles and become specialists on fast or slow surfaces. In response to the fear that dominant serving would create boring matches, and that top players make get upset in early rounds because the surface didn’t suit their style, the ATP and tournament directors have tried to make the court conditions more uniform and slow enough that players would have longer rallies.

#2 Improvement of string and racket technology. The equipment now allows players to hitter bigger returns with more topspin forcing the server to volley from below net level. This means a more difficult volley that can’t be hit with as much pace, as it has to go over the net and stay in the court. This results in better opportunities to hit a successful passing shot by the returner. The old rule of percentages was if you could hit a deep first volley directly in line with your position at the net, the server could expect to win 75% of the points. Back in the day, players couldn’t swing heavier rackets fast enough, or generate enough pace or spin to consistently get the ball down at the net player’s feet. Today, those percentages almost seem to have been reversed.

#3 Western forehands. The improved racket and string technology has allowed players to use more extreme grips that increase the amount of topspin you can create, allowing players to hit the ball even harder. With the older, heavier rackets, a player with a Western forehand was vulnerable to low, skidding balls. Now, they are able to snap their wrist and hit these difficult shots like they are ping pong balls. So the big advantage of hitting a deep volley with underspin so it stays low has been neutralized to some extent.

#4 The two-handed backhand. The two-handed backhand has eliminated two of the biggest weaknesses a big server can exploit. A fast serve could force a returner to hit the ball late and only be able to block it back, often resulting in an error or a weak return. A kick serve could force the returner to chip the return, often resulting in a high ball that could be put away easily. With the two-handed backhand, a returner can still hit topspin drives, because the shot is basically like hitting a forehand with your other hand - it’s much easier to hit late or high.

#5 The difficulty and variety of skills required. As tennis has become more competitive, players can only put so much time into each part of their game. It is much simpler to be great at serving and hitting ground strokes than it is to have to learn how to hit serves, ground strokes. volleys, approach shots and overheads. (Note: back in the days with heavier rackets, the serve return was completely different from a regular groundstroke. Due to the lack of time, players had to take very short backswings and block the ball more often. Today’s players hit most of their returns exactly the same as their ground stokes by standing much deeper in the court.) A lot of players prefer to come to the net and hit a topspin forehand volley because it is a familiar shot. If you can force them to hit a backhand volley or a low volley off either side, they make errors you would expect to see from some recreational park player.

With the lower success rate of the serve and volley, players have no incentive to learn all these extra skills. Coaches no longer try to teach the tactic. And over time, much of this knowledge will be lost, as today’s players become tomorrow’s coaches.

How come Novak Djokovic doesn't have the massive fan following compared to the likes of Rafael Nadal or Roger Federer despite being the world no. 1 for most of the past 3 seasons?

OK. Now since the facts have checked out let’s grasp the logic.

1. Where are you coming from?

Novak Djokovic is from Serbia, a country whose tennis pedigree can safely be said to have started from the generation of Novak himself. Serbia doesn’t boast of any household names before him. Ana Ivanovic, Jelena Jankovic, Viktor Troicki, Janko Tipsarevic, Jelena Dokic are more or less the same generation as Novak. No offence but when Novak came into the picture, tennis fans around the world must have been like “Oh he’s from Serbia! Let’s look up where that is.”

“But, it's because we have a harder way to succeed in life as Serbs because of the past that we had and because of the history that we had. We have to dig deeper and we have to do much more in order to be seen and to be spotted. The history of our country is cruel.”

Roger Federer comes from Switzerland which was already all over the tennis map thanks to Martina Hingis. He benefits enormously from this and largely inherits the same fan following as that of Martina, which incidentally happens to be all of the tennis world. I mean who didn’t love the Swiss Miss?
If you’re a tennis fan then let’s just not discuss the pedigree of Spain alright?

2. What era are you playing in?

You come in after two of the greatest players of all time have already established their rivalry. These two are the most polarising of tennis players. You can be a Federer fan or a Rafa fan but not both. To create a space of your own in that era is next to impossible. It’s like bringing in a new soft drink brand and trying to grab the market share from Pepsi and Coca Cola. Fancy that.

3. What do you bring to the table?

Federer is the old school charmer who plays classic in the midst of the modern power game, gliding over the court and never breaking a sweat. A gentleman who likes to wear a cream jacket at Wimbledon and maintains a likeable demeanour at all times on and off the court.
Nadal is the ultimate fighter - all blood, sweat and grit, running to fetch every ball on every point and blasting it with all the power that he can muster across the court. Never mind the grunt and the unabashed show of emotions. A galdiator who wears his heart on his sleeve.
Djokovic who? The nice guy who sits with the ball boy and offers him his drink during the rain break or the obnoxious snob complaining and yelling at ball boys and linesmen alike and disrespecting the reporters at press conferences? The emotional and impassioned warrior who tears his shirts and thumps his chest or the joker who imitates fellow players and commentators and makes everyone giggle? Or the stone cold assassin who ruthlessly dispatches opponents after opponents? You see it’s difficult to identify with Djokovic for a new tennis fan.

And now why you should love Novak Djokovic (if you don’t already).

When you think of Serbia what else can you think of? I mean, really.

“People look up to me as somebody who is able to switch the image of our country from negative to positive.”

The seasonal fan jumps into a bandwagon and it’s easy to jump in the popular bandwagon. Very few fans actually begin to follow a player before the limelight shines brightly on him. For example, not many people know of the hardships that he has overcome.

“I have been through two wars in my 24 years, and I know what it's like to be without anything, to see the bombs flying above your head.”

The war in former Yugoslavia and the bombing of Belgrade meant that, for almost three months, Djokovic used to practise on a different court each day based on whether or not there was already a bombing nearby in the previous days. So basically, Djokovic was ready to risk his life for the love of tennis. Sometimes he was forced to have training sessions in a drained out swimming pool. Acceptable tennis courts were scarce, and it was tough to come by with high-quality equipment. His family would spend a few hours in the middle of each night in the basement in order to survive the bombings. Djokovic has said that the hardships of war drove him to pursue tennis with even greater determination.

The Serbian has had to battle a myriad of health setbacks and played through enormous physical difficulties to reach the top. Djokovic was suffering from respiratory problems and recurring illness, which included sports-induced asthma, chest pains and chronic fatigue which saw him frequently collapse on court (for example during his first US Open, playing against Gael Monfils).

In 2008, Andy Roddick made fun of the fact that he so often fell ill. "Two injured ankles, hip .  .  . knee .  .  . cramps, bird flu, anthrax, SARS, common cough and cold" he jeered, while Roger Federer remarked "I think he's a joke, you know, when it comes down to his injuries."

Djokovic says his professional and personal low came during an Australian Open match in January 2010 when he collapsed while playing against Frenchman Jo-Wilfried Tsonga.

Novak’s doctor, Dr Igor Cetojevic found that he was very sensitive to gluten, a protein present in wheat, one of the most common foods in Novak’s diet. On Cetojevic's advice, Novak eliminated gluten from his diet, which meant cutting out bread and pasta, which are staples of the Serbian diet, as well as his beloved pizza. All this for the love of tennis.

In 2013, Djokovic was devastated by the death of first coach Jelena Gencic (during the 3rd round of the French Open against Dimitrov). Djokovic broke down in the locker room when his team broke the news to him. He was very close to her. He first met Gencic when he was six years old. She had once said that Novak was the greatest talent since Monica Seles (also coached by Gencic). Whether she was encouraging his interest in classical music, Pushkin's poetry or languages, Gencic always tried to develop his mind as much as his tennis.

He said to me: ‘Do you think I will win trophies like this one day?’ I told him: ‘Of course – and you will need a big house to put them all in.’

French Open: Tributes paid to coach who first saw talent in Novak

“The tradition in Serbia has always been team sports - football, basketball, handball, volleyball and water polo, individual sports are not supported. It was very unusual for a boy to play tennis in my country.”

“What I learned as a young child continues to have a tremendous impact on my life today. I want to help children in Serbia and around the world so they can realize their dreams.Those born into poverty or on the margins of society require our extra support to realize their dreams.”

“When the last point is done, we are humans. Give your opponent a hug and say, ‘great fight,’ and that’s all.”

孟秋，餘暑未消，時見涼風，費牛、納豆、德雞相約於城郊長亭圍坐。牛曰：今日唯六耳，更無他人旁聽，吾等可暢抒己見，有何疑問盡可提之，毋拘也。
豆曰：牛兄溫網奪魁，可喜可賀，但不知牛兄於溫網草地如此之牛，何故？
牛曰：草地者，球速快，彈跳低，反應需敏，好球方可出之。吾之揮拍幅度低而應變迅，故能成矣。吾觀二位揮拍均蓄力充分而幅度大，蓄力充，則慣性強而調整難，幅度大，則應變慢；電光火石，瞬息萬變，時機稍遜即逝，故不甚理想也。吾之同胞襪子同為單反，草地欠佳，無他，揮拍幅度之故也。
豆拍面門而悟，又問曰：美網球速甚於溫，然牛兄成績不及溫，又為何故？
牛笑曰：美網球速雖速，球之彈跳卻高也。豆，汝之法網獨步天下，堅不可摧，可有取勝之秘匙否？
豆聞之，整衣而理鬢，少頃，曰：法網者，紅土也。紅者，朱也，正色也。土者，五行居於中也。故紅土者，場地之正統也。土之性，溫柔敦厚，厚載萬物，不極不烈，中道也。吾觀網球者，無他，攻守也。吾以麒麟臂拉上旋，敵難攻也；吾以飛毛腿奔之，滑步停之，吾可守也。敵難攻，吾可守，敵必急，急則躁，躁則誤，或失誤送分，或回球出淺，吾可一擊制勝，或貿然上網，吾可穿之。如此，法網十冠，蓋非無由也。
牛聞之默然。豆旁顧雞曰：雞，何故無言？心莫有慮？言汝之澳網何如？
雞思之有間，曰：澳網者，慢硬也，球速不及溫而非慢，彈跳不及法而非低，無全能之攻防技術，難定也。善用兵者，非在於殺敵之眾，而在自損之寡，敵不可製我，則我必可製敵，以吾之不可勝，以待敵之可勝。運之、控之、調之、擊之，全局盡在股掌之間矣，攻，則九天之上；守，則九地之下。吾若不敗於己，安能敗於人？
牛、豆皆讚之。
牛曰：吾等入網球圈，皆逾十載也，於貨殖、名位、榮譽皆不缺矣，然仍堅守不退，緣何？二位賢弟吾不知，愚兄所守者無他，愛之也。愛之，則移情；移情，則性誠；性誠，則志明；志明，則意堅，意堅，則顛簸造次均不退也。二位賢弟可否？
豆曰：吾視球場，戰場也；球賽，戰鬥也。天命我於戰士，則吾軀若康健無恙，比全心全力投於戰鬥也。投戰，必全心全力爭勝也。勝者晉級，生也；敗者出局，死也。兵者，死生之地，大事也，不可不重也。吾為求勝，可不惜一切，血流賽場亦戰，從不言棄也。故吾觀網球，以贏球為念，除此無所求也。
豆言罷，目露寒光。牛聞之，微微一笑，轉而言雞曰：雞，爾何如？
雞曰：或異於豆兄也。先聖有云：仁者愛人。賽場失利者，首垂、氣落、淚淌，何忍觀之也！惻隱之心，人皆有之，皆為職業球員，深知養家之不易也，己所不欲勿施於人，何必劍拔弩張，兵戎相見？先王之道，和為斯美也，以和為貴，一團和氣，與人同樂，以球會友，豈不妙哉？以攻勝者，憑天時也；以守勝者，依地利也，以德勝者，得人和也。天時不如地利，地利不如人和，百戰百勝，不如不戰而屈人之兵，不戰即可勝，何必自損八百，血灑球場？
牛聞雞言，喟然而歎，曰：吾與雞也！
沉默少間，牛曰：盍不各言爾志，明歲有何期？
豆整衣冠理鬢曰：他不敢求，紅土必守耳！
雞曰：奪澳、爭法、窺溫、待美，攬大師，收五百，獲二百五，攏年終，試看明歲網壇，是誰家之天下！
豆、雞合而問牛：兄有何志？
牛方欲言，機鈴乎響，蓋米後喚之也。牛慚言：家內催歸，必有切急，恕難相陪矣，他日有暇再約，吾須速去也。
豆、雞笑曰：牛嫂之喚，豈可強留？
豆、雞送牛於長亭外，各散。

牛聞之默然。豆旁顧雞曰：雞，何故無言？心莫有慮？言汝之澳網何如？
雞思之有間，曰：澳網者，慢硬也，球速不及溫而非慢，彈跳不及法而非低，無全能之攻防技術，難定也。善用兵者，非在於殺敵之眾，而在自損之寡，敵不可製我，則我必可製敵，以吾之不可勝，以待敵之可勝。運之、控之、調之、擊之，全局盡在股掌之間矣，攻，則九天之上；守，則九地之下。吾若不敗於己，安能敗於人？
牛、豆皆讚之。
牛曰：吾等入網球圈，皆逾十載也，於貨殖、名位、榮譽皆不缺矣，然仍堅守不退，緣何？二位賢弟吾不知，愚兄所守者無他，愛之也。愛之，則移情；移情，則性誠；性誠，則志明；志明，則意堅，意堅，則顛簸造次均不退也。二位賢弟可否？
豆曰：吾視球場，戰場也；球賽，戰鬥也。天命我於戰士，則吾軀若康健無恙，比全心全力投於戰鬥也。投戰，必全心全力爭勝也。勝者晉級，生也；敗者出局，死也。兵者，死生之地，大事也，不可不重也。吾為求勝，可不惜一切，血流賽場亦戰，從不言棄也。故吾觀網球，以贏球為念，除此無所求也。
豆言罷，目露寒光。牛聞之，微微一笑，轉而言雞曰：雞，爾何如？
雞曰：或異於豆兄也。先聖有云：仁者愛人。賽場失利者，首垂、氣落、淚淌，何忍觀之也！惻隱之心，人皆有之，皆為職業球員，深知養家之不易也，己所不欲勿施於人，何必劍拔弩張，兵戎相見？先王之道，和為斯美也，以和為貴，一團和氣，與人同樂，以球會友，豈不妙哉？以攻勝者，憑天時也；以守勝者，依地利也，以德勝者，得人和也。天時不如地利，地利不如人和，百戰百勝，不如不戰而屈人之兵，不戰即可勝，何必自損八百，血灑球場？
牛聞雞言，喟然而歎，曰：吾與雞也！
沉默少間，牛曰：盍不各言爾志，明歲有何期？
豆整衣冠理鬢曰：他不敢求，紅土必守耳！
雞曰：奪澳、爭法、窺溫、待美，攬大師，收五百，獲二百五，攏年終，試看明歲網壇，是誰家之天下！
豆、雞合而問牛：兄有何志？
牛方欲言，機鈴乎響，蓋米後喚之也。牛慚言：家內催歸，必有切急，恕難相陪矣，他日有暇再約，吾須速去也。
豆、雞笑曰：牛嫂之喚，豈可強留？
豆、雞送牛於長亭外，各散。

Who can beat Nadal on clay?
Vinay Venkatesh
Vinay Venkatesh, A Nole fan, but mostly a tennis fan
Updated Sun

Rafael Nadal has a staggering 91.69% on winning percentage on clay. It means to say that of the 409 career matches he had on clay, he has lost only 34 of them. That is an impeccable record making him undoubtedly the greatest clay courter to have ever played the game.

As far as who can beat him, Novak Djokovic would come close. They have had some epic encounters on clay. I clearly remember the first one worth noting and this was in Hamburg in 2008 .

Nadal won it 7–5, 2–6, 6-2 and it was the beginning of their new rivalry.

The next one was a colossal encounter in Madrid 2009 at the semi finals. To this day, it remains to be the longest 3 set match with tie breakers and lasted 4 hours and 3 mins. Nadal was the home town favorite and he had to dig really deep. Djokovic threw everything at him and he even had 3 match points. In the end, Nadal prevailed 3–6, 7–6, 7–6.

The next one was of course at the semi finals of the 2013 Roland Garros. A marathon encounter of 4 hours and 37 minutes which left with Nadal once again victorious in 5 grueling sets 6-4, 3-6, 6-1, 6-7, 9-7. Though Djokovic was inconsolable in the end, it was a tennis match of the highest quality.

Djokovic did get his revenge when he beat Nadal for the first and only time so far at Roland Garros in his banner year of 2015 in straight sets with a 7-5, 6-3, 6-1 scoreline. It was relatively a straight forward affair given it was not Nadal’s best year.

Djokovic and Nadal have a celebrated rivalry with Djokovic leading 26–24 in their head to head encounters as of August 2017, which may only be shadowed a little by the Federer -Nadal rivalry. Given their similarities in age, the most physical duo never stops to entertain us. On clay, they have a 15–7 head to head in favor of Nadal.

The stats speak for themselves. Nadal is almost unstoppable on clay and if anyone comes close to beating him, it would be Novak Djokovic. Roger Federer would come in a close contender after that.

Worth to mention (not a clay court match) Australian Open 2012 final (longest finals in the history of game) between these two which Djokovic won 5–7 6–4 6–2 6–7 7–5 in a marathon classic of 5 hours and 53 minutes.

吳易昺成為亞洲第2年輕拿到atp挑戰賽級別冠軍的選手，第1年輕是錦織圭17歲零5個月，吳易昺17歲11個月。也成為第三位拿下挑戰賽男單桂冠的中國大陸球員（吳迪和張擇）。

今天首盤比賽，盧彥勳率先取得破發，一度以5比3領先。不過吳易昺沒有放棄，他緊咬比分，在4比5落後時頑強破掉了對手發球胜盤局。隨後的搶七中，吳易昺在以8比6拿下後，盧彥勳選擇退賽。

這樣吳易昺就以17歲11個月的年齡成為哈薩克斯坦球員科羅勒夫後拿下挑戰賽男單冠軍最年輕的亞洲球員，也成為2013年克耶高斯、2014年茲維列夫和2015年弗里茨後，近5年內第4位在同一賽季收穫青少年大滿貫男單冠軍和挑戰賽男單冠軍的球員。

　　在最近三週豪取單打11連勝後（青少年6場，挑戰賽5場）後，吳易昺的即時排名將從496位飆升到320位左右，排在吳迪、張擇和李喆之後。

Tennis-Mladenovic continues in free fall
By Reuters | Aug 31, 2017, 12.55 AM
NEW YORK, Aug 30 ( Reuters) - Kristina Mladenovic was tipped as a French Open favourite three months ago but after a fall at Wimbledon the Frenchwoman has experienced a painful form slump that resulted in a first-round exit at the U.S. Open on Wednesday.

Mladenovic, who reached the quarter-finals in Paris on the back of an impressive claycourt campaign, suffered a knee injury at the All England Club.

Things went from bad to worse for the hard-hitting Mladenovic, who appeared toothless in three consecutive first-round defeats at U.S. Open warm-up tournaments.

"There are highs and lows in a season. I had a complicated Wimbledon where I pulled a ligament in my right knee," said 14th seed Mladenovic, who lost 6-3 6-2 to Romanian Monica Nicolescu on Wednesday.

"I needed a rest after that fall but then it was not easy to come back to form for the American tournaments.

"It's frustrating, but it's part of an athlete's life ... it's a process."

Mladenovic, who showed signs of frustration on court, refused to blame her fall.

"Wimbledon is in the past, it's not why I lost today," the 24-year-old said.

"Physically, I'm in good shape, but it was not enough. I would have needed a round or two to get things started again."

Mladenovic is confident she will soon get back to her best level as she has shaken off her injury and she will not change her schedule

　　在因膝傷而遠離賽場六個月後，2017年，費達拿連奪澳網和溫網兩項大滿貫，其復出表現令世人皆驚。在36歲的高齡，現世界第二已鎖定了一場與拉法-拿度的年終世界第一之爭，這類似於2016年梅利和祖高域的榜首之爭。

　　塞爾維亞人已經因肘傷而賽季報銷，梅利也因為持續的髖關節傷勢很可能放棄2017的賸餘比賽。

　　費達拿似的休息似乎已成為一種趨勢，如今，其他球員也更願意抽出時間來恢復他們的身體健康。但就在德穆復出之前，瑞士人卻警告他們倆，這並非對每個人都有效。

The elbow injury that has sidelined Djokovic after quarterfinal loss at Wimbledon earlier this year and knocked him out of the leading position in the ATP World Tennis ranking has been rehabilitated with non-surgical methods using Dr Eugene Blum´s proprietary exercise and rehabilitation equipment and methodology installed at the temporary facility in Monte Carlo.

"Novak is an exemplary athlete and his progress is very solid. We expect full return to competition by the end of the year," said Dr Blum, Chairman of Dr Blum Clinics and Co-founder of Pendex.
Dr Eugene Blum, a renowned rehabilitation expert uses methods of biomechanical rehabilitation, techniques and proprietary exercise equipment to address a broad spectrum of complex health and injury related situations.

The goal of Djokovic’s rehabilitation program is broader that reinstatement of normal biomechanics of the elbow and aims to bring Djokovic to peak physical condition by the start of 2018 year.

Dudi Sela retires from Shenzhen due to religious reasons
30 Sep 2017 - View: 604
by Gatto Luigi

Home news Tennis Stories

Curious retirement for Dudi Sela. The World No. 77 suddenly walked away from the court during his quarter-finals match against Alexandr Dolgopolov in the ATP 250 Shenzhen, not because of injuries but religious reasons. The Israeli player retired during the second game of the third set on 6-3 4-6 1-0 when Yom Kippur, one of the most important days of the year for Jews, was taking place, which is characterized by forgiveness and abstinence towards some of the things that are routinary for a normal person.
